# Groundwork

> A personal shelf of interactive, from-first-principles technology write-ups — each one a single self-contained webpage with animated demos, worked examples, and live calculations, not just prose. Built and maintained by Rahul.

Groundwork articles are designed to be read by both people and AI assistants. Every article has a plain-Markdown version (linked below) intended for pasting into an AI chat as context — use it to ask follow-up questions, request quizzes, or have a section re-explained a different way. The interactive HTML versions include live sliders, canvas/SVG demos, and equations, none of which survive a plain-text scrape, so the Markdown versions exist specifically to give AI systems accurate, complete context without needing to parse JavaScript.

## Articles

- [Machine Learning from First Principles](https://cloudsnativeai.cloud/articles/ml-fundamentals.html): Linear regression and gradient descent taught through one worked example (predicting house prices), covering the model, the loss function, derivatives, gradients, gradient descent, the training loop, a live worked example, the loss-surface visualization, and generalization to logistic regression and neural networks. [Markdown version](https://cloudsnativeai.cloud/articles/ml-fundamentals.md) for AI context.
- [Cloud Native to Dapr](https://cloudsnativeai.cloud/articles/Cloud_Native_to_Dapr.html): An architecture deep-dive from why cloud native exists, through containers and Kubernetes, to the sidecar runtime (Dapr) that lifts cross-cutting concerns — discovery, state, messaging, secrets, retries, telemetry — out of application code.
- [Cloud Native Applications, from First Principles](https://cloudsnativeai.cloud/articles/cloud-native-fundamentals_1.html): Why running in the cloud isn't the same as being cloud native, and how containers, Kubernetes, and a service mesh turn a fragile monolith into something that scales and heals itself.

## Optional

- [Homepage](https://cloudsnativeai.cloud/): card-grid index of all Groundwork articles.
